_comment: Genuine, demonstrated output degradations that are NOT regressions to repair. A degradation belongs here ONLY if (a) it persists after a Regle-F repair attempt (demonstrated), or (b) it depends on a genuinely-external resource the cluster cannot provision (paid service with no creds, live testnet, host-deployed GenAI stack). Everything else must be repaired + re-run, never consecrated (cf #3660 / audit-output-health-axis). Each entry needs reason + evidence_url + demonstrated_by.
_policy_installable_tools: An INSTALLABLE tool is NEVER allowlist-eligible. A TOOL_MISSING / 'non disponible' / 'Skipped' marker for a JVM/Tweety bridge, SWI-Prolog, MiniZinc, clingo, Graphviz/dot, or any conda/pip package in OUR OWN committed notebooks is an ENVIRONMENT REGRESSION, not a 'genuine skip' (user mandate 2026-06-20). As notebook owners our execution environments must be impeccable: install the tool (Regle F) + re-execute faithfully. Fallbacks are for THIRD PARTIES with env problems, never an excuse for the owner to commit a degraded output.
_policy_faithful_or_handoff: The allowlist is NOT permission to commit degraded outputs. Even for a genuinely-external (b) case: re-execute with the FULL faithful target environment, or do NOT touch the notebook at all (user mandate 2026-06-20). An agent that cannot faithfully reproduce a notebook's target execution environment MUST signal and hand the notebook to a machine/agent that can -- it must NOT re-run it and overwrite the expected outputs with degraded ones. Touching a notebook = re-running it on a faithful env -> real outputs, or leaving it untouched.

_schema.path: repo-relative notebook path suffix (matched by endswith)
_schema.cause: single cause tag, list of tags, or '*' for any
_schema.cell_signature: optional human note on which cell
_schema.reason: why this degradation is genuine, not recoverable

acknowledged.cell_signature: WFC solve returns FEASIBLE not OPTIMAL
acknowledged.reason: Demonstrated genuine: FEASIBLE persists at 20/60/120s solve budgets; raising the timeout does NOT reach OPTIMAL. Honest caveat is defensible (not a recoverable degradation).

Experimentation ARCHIVE by-design (paired with research.ipynb REFERENCE template). The 13 NameError/KeyError cells are leftover residuals from iterative prototyping on QC Cloud where cells reference variables defined/renamed in later iterations. This is NOT a degraded deliverable: it is the preserved trace of experimentation. The canonical REFERENCE notebook (research.ipynb, same dir, now flagged metadata.qc_reference=True) is the clean by-design template. Archive is committed for traceability of the Crypto-MultiCanal channel research, not as an executable pedagogical output. Greenlit by ai-01 (2026-06-20) as Option A partner-course policy.
